raghuveera-gadyam: Composed by Vedanta Deshika, one of the greatest Vaishnava Acharyas
Narrates Rama's complete story in powerful prose form
Calls on Rama's might to overcome enemies and obstacles
Each 'Jaya Jaya' invocation is a powerful declaration of Rama's triumph
Ideal for Ram Navami, Dussehra, and before any competitive endeavor
Invokes the blessing of divine courage, strength, and fearlessness
rahu-sahasranama: Traditionally recited for removal of rahu dosha and kala sarpa dosha completely
Traditionally recited for removal of fear during rahu kaal and eclipse periods
Traditionally recited for removal of mental disorders and psychological afflictions
Traditionally invoked for protection from black magic, spirits, and negative energies
Invokes success in foreign travels and overseas ventures
Recited on Saturdays to win Rahu's grace and lasting devotion
rahu-stotram: Five-verse prayer to Rahu for steadfast devotion and inner growth
Honours Rahu's shadow-planet power over karma and hidden forces
Steadies the mind and pacifies Rahu's unsettling planetary influence
Eases Rahu-related fears, afflictions, and sudden misfortune
Ideal for Rahu graha shanti and worship
ram-stuti: Traditionally recited for removal of the fear of worldly existence (bhav-bhaya)
Invokes devotion and love for lord rama
Cleanses the devotee's mind and heart through Rama's holy name
Invokes the blessing of peace and spiritual bliss
Traditionally recited for removal of lust, anger, and other inner enemies
rama-ashtakam: Eight verses to Rama that wash away sins and purify the soul
Invokes eternal liberation (moksha) to regular readers
Invokes the blessing of infinite happiness and spiritual bliss
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of desires of devoted readers
Traditionally recited for removal of delusion and grants true knowledge
Provides the complete grace of Lord Rama
ramayana-chopai: Encapsulates the essence of the entire Ramcharitmanas
Invokes the fruit of reading the full ramayana in condensed form
Invokes the blessing of spiritual wisdom and devotion to lord rama
Traditionally recited for removal of ignorance, sorrow, and fear
Suited to short daily paath when a full Ramayana reading is not possible
ramraksha-stotra: Traditionally invoked to establish a divine protective shield around the devotee
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins including great sins (mahapatakas)
Grants victory in every undertaking and contest through Rama's strength
Traditionally recited for removal of fear of death and yamadutas
Draws Rama's grace as wealth, prosperity, and abundance of every kind
Provides protection from enemies and evil forces
Invokes the supreme grace of lord rama
ratha-saptami-stotram: Specific stotram for Ratha Saptami festival
Recited on Ratha Saptami to dissolve sins of past lifetimes
Traditionally recited for removal of chronic diseases and deep-seated grief
Invokes freedom from all sins when observed with bathing and worship
Leads to Surya-loka (solar realm) after life
rudra-gayatri: Invokes the fierce protective energy of Lord Rudra
Traditionally recited for removal of fear, negative energies, and evil influences
Kindles Rudra's gift of courage, inner strength, and fearlessness
Invokes protection from untimely death and calamities
Purifies the mind and leads to spiritual enlightenment
rudra-suktam: Ancient Rigvedic hymn to Rudra, the fierce yet benevolent deity
Invokes Rudra's healing and protective powers
Traditionally recited for removal of diseases, pestilence, and all afflictions
Draws Rudra's strength to make the devotee brave and fearless
Protects family, cattle, and all possessions
Invokes the blessing of rudra's medicinal grace for health and longevity
rudram-chamakam-excerpt: Yajurvedic chant petitioning Rudra-Shiva for life's manifold boons
Honours Shiva as both fierce Rudra and gracious giver of plenty
Calls down Shiva's protection and the peace of an auspicious life
Recited to gain prosperity, abundance, and freedom from want
Suited to Maha Shivaratri observance and Monday Shiva worship
sai-ashtakam: Invokes the blessing of the grace and blessings of shirdi sai baba
Eight verses to Sai Baba that cleanse sins and purify the devotee
Traditionally recited for removal of poverty, suffering and obstacles
Invokes healing from physical and mental ailments
Leads to spiritual union (sayujya) with Sai
saraswati-kavacham: Provides divine armor of protection from Goddess Saraswati
Grants command over scripture, the arts, and every branch of learning
Invokes the blessing of victory in debates, examinations, and intellectual pursuits
Enhances speech, communication, and oratory skills
Protects the devotee in all ten directions through divine names
Invokes the supreme eternal knowledge (para vidya)
shailputri-stotram: Invokes the first form of Navdurga for Navratri pratipada worship
Activates the Muladhara (root) chakra for spiritual awakening
Draws Shailputri's grace for wealth, prosperity, and abundance
Invokes the blessing of good health, longevity, and freedom from diseases
Traditionally recited for removal of great delusion (mahamoha) and grants liberation
Essential for beginning Navratri sadhana on the first day
shakambhari-stotram: Invokes Shakambhari Devi, the nourisher who creates food during famine
Recited to banish poverty, hunger, and every form of scarcity
Invokes agricultural prosperity and abundant harvest (especially for farmers)
Traditionally invoked for protection from drought, famine, and natural calamities
Invokes the blessing of food security and nourishment to all beings
Devotee never faces destitution or suffering by Shakambhari's grace
shani-chalisa: Traditionally recited for removal of the malefic effects of shani sade sati and shani dhaiya
Traditionally invoked for protection from planetary afflictions of saturn
Traditionally recited for removal of obstacles and suffering caused by karmic debts
Invokes the blessing of prosperity and removes poverty
Invokes fulfillment of desires when recited on saturdays
Shields the devotee from accidents and untimely death by Shani's grace
shani-sahasranama: Traditionally recited for removal of shani dosha, sadhesati, and kantaka shani effects
Traditionally recited for removal of fear and suffering caused by saturn transits
Invokes patience, endurance, and spiritual discipline
Traditionally recited for removal of poverty and grants stable prosperity
Invokes the blessing of karmic purification and righteous conduct
Chanted on Shani Jayanti to deepen devotion to Saturn's lord
shankaracharya-ashtakam: Eight verses honouring Adi Shankaracharya, the Advaita master
Salutes Shankara's role as guru who reveals non-dual truth
Invokes the guru's grace for clarity of mind and right discernment
Recited to dissolve ignorance and ripen merit toward liberation
Fitting for Guru Purnima and Shankaracharya Jayanti observance
shanmukha-gayatri: Three Gayatri mantras dedicated to Lord Shanmukha (Kartikeya)
Calls on Shanmukha's spear to scatter enemies and dark forces
Invokes all siddhis and spiritual accomplishments
Ideal for those facing challenges and opposition
Contains dhyanam, mula mantra and stuti
shiv-kavach: Wraps the devotee in Shiva's divine armour against every harm
Guards all body parts through different forms of Shiva
Provides protection from all eight directions
Traditionally recited for removal of fear of enemies, evil spirits, and negativity
Recited daily, it grants the sincere devotee's heartfelt wishes
Invokes the rare merit that even devas find difficult to obtain
Most powerful when recited daily after Shiva puja
shiv-mahimna-stotram: Considered one of the greatest Shiva stotras ever composed
Invokes deep philosophical understanding of shiva's nature
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of sins and karmic obstacles
Invokes the blessing of liberation (moksha) upon the devoted reciter
Pleases Lord Shiva immensely — composed by celestial Gandharva Pushpadanta
Traditionally associated with prosperity, peace, and divine protection
Daily recitation during Shravan month yields immense merit
Dispels ignorance and opens the reciter to Shiva's wisdom
shiv-panchakshara-stotram: Unfolds the meaning of the five syllables Na-Ma-Shi-Va-Ya
Invokes entry to shivaloka after death
Invokes the blessing of purity of mind and body
Powerful mantra-based stotra for daily recitation
Recited to burn away sins and the residue of past karma
Traditionally associated with deep devotional connection with lord shiva
Recitation near a Shiva temple multiplies the merit
shiv-shadakshara-stotram: Meditates on the six syllables Om-Na-Ma-Shi-Va-Ya one by one
Each verse meditates on one letter — Om, Na, Ma, Shi, Va, Ya
The six-syllable mantra is Shiva's most sacred formula
Invokes both worldly desires (kama) and liberation (moksha)
Traditionally recited for removal of great sins (mahapapa) through the power of the mantra
Regular recitation near a Shiva temple grants Shivaloka
shiv-stuti: Powerful prayer for complete surrender to Lord Shiva
Traditionally recited for removal of fears, sorrows, and worldly afflictions
Awakens spiritual wisdom and the stillness of inner peace
Traditionally recited for removal of the cycle of birth and death
Invokes the blessing of divine grace and protection of shiva
Ideal stuti for daily recitation by devotees
Invokes Shiva as the Supreme Being beyond all attributes
shiva-ashtakam-prabhu: Invokes the supreme blessings of lord shiva
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of sins and worldly attachments
Invokes the blessing of spiritual wisdom and inner peace
Traditionally invoked for protection from all calamities and evil forces
Recited on Maha Shivaratri and Shravan Mondays for Shiva's grace
